CHROMA, this track was dropped on October 21, making its debut on Tyler's YouTube channel before hitting streaming platforms just an hour later. The music video vividly illustrates the album's protagonist, St. Chroma, grappling with overwhelming paranoia. As he frantically speeds away from perceived threats Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Noid"—short for “Paranoid”—serves as the lead single from Tyler, The Creator’s highly anticipated eighth studio album, CHROMAKOPIA. Following the first teaser, ST. CHROMA, this track was dropped on October 21, making its debut on Tyler's YouTube channel before hitting streaming platforms just an hour later. The music video vividly illustrates the album's protagonist, St. Chroma, grappling with overwhelming paranoia. As he frantically speeds away from perceived threats, the imagery intensifies: he imagines a menacing car tailing him and envisions a figure brandishing a gun inside his own home, blurring the line between reality and delusion. Through this visual narrative, Tyler captures the essence of fear and isolation, drawing listeners deeper into the mind of St. Chroma. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “Rah Tah Tah” is one of the few songs teased in a CHROMAKOPIA promotional video, uploaded to Tyler’s channel on October 22, 2024, where a 12-second part of the song was featured in a medley of different CHROMAKOPIA snippets. This track portrays the chaotic side of the album, featuring Tyler’s well-known aggressive rapping style, with the production following suit and sounding just as menacing. +28 Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “Good Luck, Babe!“ entails a relationship Chappell Roan had with a woman who struggled with compulsory heterosexuality, the conflict eventually causing their split. Roan teased this song a month prior to its release by sharing a minute-long snippet. Additionally, she further teased the track by performing it live before its initial release date. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “BIRDS OF A FEATHER” is one of the standout tracks from Billie Eilish’s third album HIT ME HARD AND SOFT. The second single became a massive success, gaining widespread acclaim for its emotional depth and resonating with a global audience. It eventually became Billie Eilish’s first song to top the Billboard Global 200 chart, especially after her performance during the 2024 Summer Olympics closing ceremony. Thematically, the song delves into the longing for deep love and lasting connection Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “BIRDS OF A FEATHER” is one of the standout tracks from Billie Eilish’s third album HIT ME HARD AND SOFT. The second single became a massive success, gaining widespread acclaim for its emotional depth and resonating with a global audience. It eventually became Billie Eilish’s first song to top the Billboard Global 200 chart, especially after her performance during the 2024 Summer Olympics closing ceremony. Thematically, the song delves into the longing for deep love and lasting connection, exploring the idea of kindred spirits finding one another. The track is known for its emotive lyrics and Billie’s impassioned vocal delivery, making it one of the most heartfelt and memorable pieces from the album. Musically, “BIRDS OF A FEATHER” features a powerful blend of impactful songwriting and rich production. It has been praised for its ability to evoke strong emotional reactions, largely due to Billie’s ability to connect deeply with her audience through her lyrics and voice. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “I Killed You” centers around the complex relationship Black people have with their hair, navigating a world that often frowns upon natural styles. This pressure leads many to “kill it” by cutting it all off, capturing the struggle between self-identity and societal expectations. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “ST. CHROMA” marks a new era of Tyler, The Creator music, acting as CHROMAKOPIA’s introduction. It was teased on October 16, 2024 with the reveal of the album, featuring additional vocals from Daniel Caesar. Following the brief teaser’s release, Caesar wrote to his Instagram Story: Did some vocals on this, very proud. Can’t wait for you all to hear the whole record. In context of CHROMAKOPIA, the YouTube bio of “ST CHROMA” is simply the number eight, referring to Tyler reaching his eighth studio album. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: The song reflects on themes of relationships, fame, and personal growth, which are typical of Tyler's work. He often uses vivid stories and humor, which adds depth to his commentary on life's challenges. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “Taste” is the first track off of Sabrina Carpenter’s sixth studio album, Short n' Sweet, and was released as the highly anticipated third single with a music video featuring female actress Jenna Ortega on August 23, 2024, following hits “Espresso” & “Please Please Please”. Disco-infused & irresistibly danceable to, “Taste” serves as the album’s opener, layering risqué innuendos with revenge and betrayal, stating that even if he’s cheating, she’ll allow it as he’s always got the taste of her on his lips. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Taste” is the first track off of Sabrina Carpenter’s sixth studio album, Short n' Sweet, and was released as the highly anticipated third single with a music video featuring female actress Jenna Ortega on August 23, 2024, following hits “Espresso” & “Please Please Please”. Disco-infused & irresistibly danceable to, “Taste” serves as the album’s opener, layering risqué innuendos with revenge and betrayal, stating that even if he’s cheating, she’ll allow it as he’s always got the taste of her on his lips. The music video, much like the track, cheekily infers many dark satirical movies, such as ’Ginger Snaps‘, ’The Addams Family’, & primarily ’Death Becomes Her’, with Ortega playing Madeline, who’s played by Meryl Streep, & Carpenter playing Helen, who’s played by Goldie Hawn. “So, ‘Taste’ is one of my favorite songs off the album and it says a lot about me and a lot about other people. I think it’s sort of poking fun of myself a little bit for my mistakes and also poking fun at the, kind of like, facepalm that is life and relationship and the, sort of, situations that it puts you in, um, and it’s just making lighthearted fun at the whole concept of, you know, people that you’ve been with moving on to other people, and, you know, I think it’s a very human thing but it’s certainly fun.“ — via Kiss 108 Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: "Espresso" is a song by American singer-songwriter Sabrina Carpenter. It was released as a single on April 11, 2024. On January 16, 2024, Sabrina Carpenter was confirmed as part of the American festival Coachella, held in Indio, California. She teased a new song, ahead of her performance at the festival, via billboards that read: "She's gonna make you come... to her Coachella set!". Carpenter announced the release of "Espresso" on her social media accounts on April 8, 2024, along with its release date and cover artwork. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Espresso" is a song by American singer-songwriter Sabrina Carpenter. It was released as a single on April 11, 2024. On January 16, 2024, Sabrina Carpenter was confirmed as part of the American festival Coachella, held in Indio, California. She teased a new song, ahead of her performance at the festival, via billboards that read: "She's gonna make you come... to her Coachella set!". Carpenter announced the release of "Espresso" on her social media accounts on April 8, 2024, along with its release date and cover artwork. She captioned the post with: "Just wanted to put out a little song before Coachella". The song is resonating with fans hugely, and became one of Sabrina's biggest hits and debuts on charts. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “Die with a Smile” is the comeback single by Lady Gaga and Bruno Mars who both haven’t released music in 2 to 3 years. The song was surprise announced a day before the release on August 15th and released at 12am EST alongside the music video. Gaga confirmed on the announcement post that this song is a song to hold fans over until she releases her highly anticipated seventh studio album which she has teased through out all of 2024. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: Rosé’s song "APT," a collaboration with Bruno Mars, is inspired by a traditional Korean drinking game called "Apartment" (apateu in Korean). The song, which blends playful energy with pop-punk elements, features catchy chants that revolve around meeting at an apartment, symbolizing urban life and fun gatherings. Rosé initially had reservations about writing a song based on a drinking game, but the track’s infectious energy quickly won over her team and listeners. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: Rosé’s song "APT," a collaboration with Bruno Mars, is inspired by a traditional Korean drinking game called "Apartment" (apateu in Korean). The song, which blends playful energy with pop-punk elements, features catchy chants that revolve around meeting at an apartment, symbolizing urban life and fun gatherings. Rosé initially had reservations about writing a song based on a drinking game, but the track’s infectious energy quickly won over her team and listeners. Released on October 18, 2024, "APT" serves as a teaser for Rosé's upcoming solo album and highlights her dynamic chemistry with Bruno Mars, both musically and through their lively interactions on social media​. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: Tyler’s favorite off of Flower Boy, “See You Again” is a love song. The song features vocals from Kali Uchis, who appeared on Tyler’s 2015 album Cherry Bomb on “FIND YOUR WINGS” and “FUCKING YOUNG / PERFECT.” The person Tyler sings to on this song seems to be his ideal lover, as they only live in his fantasy and dream state. Whenever he wakes up this person disappears, and therefore he doesn’t wish to wake up. While he is very much in love with them, he hates constantly waiting and chasing. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: Tyler’s favorite off of Flower Boy, “See You Again” is a love song. The song features vocals from Kali Uchis, who appeared on Tyler’s 2015 album Cherry Bomb on “FIND YOUR WINGS” and “FUCKING YOUNG / PERFECT.” The person Tyler sings to on this song seems to be his ideal lover, as they only live in his fantasy and dream state. Whenever he wakes up this person disappears, and therefore he doesn’t wish to wake up. While he is very much in love with them, he hates constantly waiting and chasing. The song directly follows the interlude “Sometimes…” where we hear a male voice ask Shane Powers to play the song about him on Golf Radio. What comes next is “See You Again.” This implies that the song is likely about a man. Tyler brought up the song two times during a self-published interview titled FLOWER BOY: a conversation that he released on his YouTube channel. “See You Again” was certified platinum by the RIAA on August 5, 2019. It was Tyler’s first platinum certification of any track in his discography. The track was later certified 2x platinum on March 23, 2021. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “Tomorrow” is the 10th track on Tyler’s album, "CHROMAKOPIA", Tyler was always known for putting two-part songs on the 10th track. Not only has Tyler dropped after a year prior to his "CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST: The Estate Sale", but he also decides to not put a two-part song on the album. The first teaser for this song was shared on the Chromakopia TikTok page in a 46 second snippet uploaded on October 24, 2024. This song has a very tender Tyler inflection Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Tomorrow” is the 10th track on Tyler’s album, "CHROMAKOPIA", Tyler was always known for putting two-part songs on the 10th track. Not only has Tyler dropped after a year prior to his "CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST: The Estate Sale", but he also decides to not put a two-part song on the album. The first teaser for this song was shared on the Chromakopia TikTok page in a 46 second snippet uploaded on October 24, 2024. This song has a very tender Tyler inflection, singing in mostly a high key naturally rather than pitching as seen on "IGOR", but there’s still pitching on some vocals. Read more on Last.fm. wiki.summary: “Guess featuring Billie Eilish” is a remix of ”Guess” off of the deluxe version of Charli’s seventh studio album, ’BRAT’. Charli first teased the remix on the 31st of July, captioning the post ’Guess ?’; in which, much like the cheeky lyrics in the original song, Charli cheekily wants her fans to guess who is collaborating on the remix. Although at that time, the collaborator’s identity remained ambiguous, it was speculated by many that American singer & songwriter Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Guess featuring Billie Eilish” is a remix of ”Guess” off of the deluxe version of Charli’s seventh studio album, ’BRAT’. Charli first teased the remix on the 31st of July, captioning the post ’Guess ?’; in which, much like the cheeky lyrics in the original song, Charli cheekily wants her fans to guess who is collaborating on the remix. Although at that time, the collaborator’s identity remained ambiguous, it was speculated by many that American singer & songwriter, Billie Eilish, was to be collaborating with Charli on the song. Billie was to add further fuel to the speculation via her Instagram story; entertaining the potential of her being featured on the remix, with a question mark on her face, once again cheekily jabbing at the audience with the ‘guessing’ symbol. A day later, on August 1st, it was confirmed by Charli that Billie was the one collaborating on the track. Charli also revealed the existence of a music video coinciding with the remix that would be released at 6pm EST on the same day. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: Darling, I is the fourth track off Tyler, the Creator's 2024 album "CHROMAKOPIA". The song features vocals from Teezo Touchdown and credits from Barry White, Kamaal Ibn John Fareed (Q-Tip) and Tyler himself. The kick pattern of this song was sampled from 'Vivrant Thing' by producer and rapper Q-Tip. Read more on Last.fm.

album.title: The Rise and Fall of a Midwest Princess

wiki.summary: “HOT TO GO!“ is a single released for Chappell Roan's debut studio album, The Rise and Fall of a Midwest Princess. The song was released on August 11, 2023, alongside an official music video. Chappell shared the following about the song's inspiration in press release statements: “I wrote this song so I could live out my cheerleader fantasy! I just wanted to make something simple and silly that I could do with the audience because I’m a huge fan of audience participation. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “HOT TO GO!“ is a single released for Chappell Roan's debut studio album, The Rise and Fall of a Midwest Princess. The song was released on August 11, 2023, alongside an official music video. Chappell shared the following about the song's inspiration in press release statements: “I wrote this song so I could live out my cheerleader fantasy! I just wanted to make something simple and silly that I could do with the audience because I’m a huge fan of audience participation. Also, selfishly (and shamelessly) wanted to bounce around on stage singing a song about being hot.“ — via coup de main “I wanted a cheerleading song, I wanted something that I could do with the audience. Like, I was really jealous of the “Y.M.C.A.”. So, I made, “H-O-T-T-O-G-O”, a little dance that I can do with the audience. And fun fact, it’s actually a real cheer. But I made up my own cheer with that. But it’s fun, come to a show and I’ll teach you the dance.“ – via NME Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: "360" is a song by English singer Charli xcx. It was released on 10 May 2024 through Atlantic Records as the third single from her sixth studio album, BRAT. The accompanying music video for "360" was released on the same day as the track. It was directed by Aidan Zamiri. The music video follows the quest to find a "new, popular internet it girl". Thom Waite of Dazed compared the video to a "parallel-universe production of Euphoria or a 2020s it girl twist on Girls". Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "360" is a song by English singer Charli xcx. It was released on 10 May 2024 through Atlantic Records as the third single from her sixth studio album, BRAT. The accompanying music video for "360" was released on the same day as the track. It was directed by Aidan Zamiri. The music video follows the quest to find a "new, popular internet it girl". Thom Waite of Dazed compared the video to a "parallel-universe production of Euphoria or a 2020s it girl twist on Girls". It features appearances from Chloë Sevigny, Julia Fox, Rachel Sennott, Emma Chamberlain, Gabbriette, Alex Consani, A. G. Cook, Chloe Cherry, Isamaya Ffrench, Salem Mitchell, Hari Nef, Richie Shazam, among others. Matthew Velasco of W described the cast as "a Mount Rushmore of reining sic internet cool girls". On February 17, 2023, producer Patrik Berger posted a picture on Instagram from a studio session with Charli and Swedish singer Robyn. When questioned about it on Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen, Charli described the experience of them working together as "amazing" and hinted at the possibility of their collaboration being released before Charli's album, BRAT. On May 31, 2024, Charli released a remix version of the track featuring Robyn and the fellow Swedish musician Yung Lean. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: "Please Please Please" is a song by American singer-songwriter Sabrina Carpenter. It was released on June 6, 2024, through Island Records, as the second single from her sixth studio album, Short n' Sweet. Produced by Jack Antonoff, the pop ballad contains musical influences of disco, country, and yacht rock; lyrically, the singer instructs her boyfriend not to blunder and blemish her reputation. An instant commercial success, the song debuted at number one in Ireland Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Please Please Please" is a song by American singer-songwriter Sabrina Carpenter. It was released on June 6, 2024, through Island Records, as the second single from her sixth studio album, Short n' Sweet. Produced by Jack Antonoff, the pop ballad contains musical influences of disco, country, and yacht rock; lyrically, the singer instructs her boyfriend not to blunder and blemish her reputation. An instant commercial success, the song debuted at number one in Ireland, becoming Carpenter's second consecutive chart-topping single in that country, and has also reached the top ten in Australia, Norway, New Zealand, and the United Kingdom. Following the commercial success of "Espresso", the singer announced her upcoming sixth studio album Short n' Sweet on June 3, 2024. She simultaneously promised an upcoming surprise for her fans, which would turn out to be the single. Only two days later, Carpenter took to social media to announce the song's release and share a teaser of the accompanying music video featuring cinematic visuals set to showcase her acting skills. The clip shows her wearing a lacy red outfit in a robbery-like setting, as well as engaging in a romance with a criminal played by her real-life boyfriend Barry Keoghan. The music video for "Please Please Please" serves as a sequel to the "Espresso" music video released earlier in 2024. In the music video for "Espresso", Carpenter gets arrested at the end. “I ended the last video getting arrested, so naturally I thought it would be satisfying to start the ‘Please Please Please’ video in jail,” said the singer. “I liked the idea of falling in love with a convict and being shocked and embarrassed every time he commits crimes. I was sooo lucky to get Barry Keoghan in the video ’cause he is just magic on screen.” said the singer after the music video was released. The Irish actor is rumored to be Carpenter's off-screen boyfriend after they started being seen together in December 2023 and throughout 2024. Bardia Zeinali, the music video director, commented about the inspiration for the music video to GQ. "The concept was pretty collaborative. I had a brief from Sabrina's team – they wanted it to pick up from where “Espresso” left off and they wanted it to be about this person that Sabrina's character keeps falling for, that has this chaotic behaviour. So I just elaborated on that and started to think a bit more about specifically what happens. I liked this idea of there being a role reversal and an exchange of power towards the end. And things that were recurring, like her picking him up in the same place, same location, different day, different time.", said the director to GQ. "Pam and Tommy. Madonna and Dennis Rodman. Tarantino was a big reference point. Natural Born Killers. Bonnie and Clyde, obviously. Even Thelma & Louise. We were really looking at film references more than anything else and pop culture references in terms of what we wanted the spirit of this moment to be. Even J Lo and Ben Affleck – not to reference, but to capture that spirit. It's similar in the sense that they're this really exciting young couple, both having big moments in their respective careers, coming together and making a piece of art. But this is very much not real life – it's a story," said the director. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

name: Sympathy is a knife featuring ariana grande

wiki.summary: ‘Sympathy Is A Knife Featuring Ariana Grande’ (stylized in sentence case) is the third song from Charli xcx’s remix album Brat and it's completely different but also still brat, featuring singer-songwriter Ariana Grande. Grande’s inclusion on the remix itself was revealed via billboards in her hometown of Boca Raton, Florida, which depicted her name on top of a ‘brat’ green backdrop; a point further confirmed by Charli on October 5th when she posted a collage of all names featured on billboards. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “Pink + White” is the third track on Frank’s sophomore album Blonde. It’s produced by Pharrell Williams and features uncredited backing vocals from Beyoncé. Pharrell seemingly confirmed producing this song with an Instagram post. Pink + White #frankocean The track details memories of Frank’s youth and of someone close to Frank who passed away. He remembers this person by experiencing his life through the lens of this person who is gone. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Pink + White” is the third track on Frank’s sophomore album Blonde. It’s produced by Pharrell Williams and features uncredited backing vocals from Beyoncé. Pharrell seemingly confirmed producing this song with an Instagram post. Pink + White #frankocean The track details memories of Frank’s youth and of someone close to Frank who passed away. He remembers this person by experiencing his life through the lens of this person who is gone. As sung in the song’s opening lines, the track’s title is a reference to a sunset drive with Frank and his old friend. “Pink + White” describes the sky during the moment he is reminiscing on. Frank has used those colors in songs before—He has a song titled “White” in Odd Future’s The OF Tape Vol. 2 and a song called “Pink Matter” on Channel ORANGE. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “EARFQUAKE” is a collaboration between Tyler, The Creator and Playboi Carti. In the song, Tyler sings about a lover he depends on, who he knows he hasn’t treated the best, but needs. This is done over beautiful, cheery piano chords and heavy, distorted drums, mixing styles from his last two albums, Cherry Bomb and Flower Boy. The track’s title references a popular line from Carti’s 2015 hit single “Fetti”: "I just left Houston, I just left earf" Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “EARFQUAKE” is a collaboration between Tyler, The Creator and Playboi Carti. In the song, Tyler sings about a lover he depends on, who he knows he hasn’t treated the best, but needs. This is done over beautiful, cheery piano chords and heavy, distorted drums, mixing styles from his last two albums, Cherry Bomb and Flower Boy. The track’s title references a popular line from Carti’s 2015 hit single “Fetti”: "I just left Houston, I just left earf" Tyler dropped a music video for " EARFQUAKE" on his Youtube channel the following day after the album’s release. The video’s set is an identical set to the teaser for “WHAT’S GOOD”. It plays out as a musical audition on a daytime TV show gone wrong. Actress Tracee Ellis Ross stars in the video. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “Best Interest“ is a song by the American rapper Tyler, the Creator. It was first released on December 23, 2019 as a music video, before being released to streaming services on January 25, 2020. The song contains a sample of "Bless You With My Love" by Heaven Sent & Ecstasy. The song was labeled by some as a so-called "IGOR outtake," a song that just didn't make the cut for the rapper's fifth studio album. Read more on Last.fm.

name: Thought I Was Dead (feat. ScHoolboy Q & Santigold)

wiki.summary: On “NEW MAGIC WAND”, Tyler desperately attempts to convince someone he has feelings for to not leave him. However, the person Tyler is in love with is still thinking about their previous relationship with a woman. In the song, Tyler goes to extreme length such as threatening to kill both his love interest and his love interest’s ex-girlfriend in order to get what he wants. “NEW MAGIC WAND” was first teased in a 47-second snippet posted on Tyler’s social media platforms on May 14, 2019. Read more on Last.fm.

The song opens with grimy beat, and features a soft synth change during the bridge. The song features an introduction by comedian Jerrod Carmichael. Read more on Last.fm. wiki.summary: "Sticky" is the 8th track on Tyler, The Creator's 8th studio album "CHROMAKOPIA". This song was first revealed in full during the CHROMAKOPIA listening event on October 27, 2024, however a snippet of Lil Wayne's verse leaked a few days prior. The song features Lil Wayne, Sexyy Red and GloRilla, with Tyler and his collaborators rapping about staying true to themselves, standing up to anyone who doubts them, and embracing their success and individuality. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Sticky" is the 8th track on Tyler, The Creator's 8th studio album "CHROMAKOPIA". This song was first revealed in full during the CHROMAKOPIA listening event on October 27, 2024, however a snippet of Lil Wayne's verse leaked a few days prior. The song features Lil Wayne, Sexyy Red and GloRilla, with Tyler and his collaborators rapping about staying true to themselves, standing up to anyone who doubts them, and embracing their success and individuality. With verses about self-worth, taking on haters and flaunting their achievements, each artist brings a bold attitude, humor, and clever lines about life, fame, and relationships. The repeated line, “Better find a mop, it’s gettin’ sticky,” is a playful way of saying things are messy but they’re ready for it. The latter half of the song samples Young Buck’s "Get Buck". Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “Timeless” is the second single by The Weeknd for his upcoming album, Hurry Up Tomorrow. This time, featuring rapper Playboi Carti. The song was released on September 27th, 2024 and marks the duo’s second collaboration since The Weeknd’s June 2023 track, “Popular (Music from the HBO Original Series)” with Madonna. The song was performed live during The Weeknd’s concert in São Paulo on September 7, 2024, with Playboi Carti also performing on the stage. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Timeless” is the second single by The Weeknd for his upcoming album, Hurry Up Tomorrow. This time, featuring rapper Playboi Carti. The song was released on September 27th, 2024 and marks the duo’s second collaboration since The Weeknd’s June 2023 track, “Popular (Music from the HBO Original Series)” with Madonna. The song was performed live during The Weeknd’s concert in São Paulo on September 7, 2024, with Playboi Carti also performing on the stage. A (AI) demo of the song leaked on September 25th, 2024, the same day, The Weeknd takes to his Instagram to post a photo of him with Carti with the caption stating: TIMELESS // TOMORROW NIGHT Shortly after the post, Carti reposts the image of the two on his Instagram story to build up even more hype. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

name: Take Your Mask Off (feat. Daniel Caesar & LaToiya Williams)

wiki.summary: “Take Your Mask Off” is the 9th song on Tyler, The Creator’s 8th studio album “CHROMAKOPIA.” The song was first teased in a “CHROMAKOPIA VINYL” promotional video, uploaded to Tyler’s channel on October 22, 2024. A short part of this song was featured in among the other “CHROMAKOPIA” snippets. It follows a pattern of exposing the “masks” people wear in their lives to hide their true thoughts or feelings, from a thug not wanting to be one but doing so to fit in and seem cool Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Take Your Mask Off” is the 9th song on Tyler, The Creator’s 8th studio album “CHROMAKOPIA.” The song was first teased in a “CHROMAKOPIA VINYL” promotional video, uploaded to Tyler’s channel on October 22, 2024. A short part of this song was featured in among the other “CHROMAKOPIA” snippets. It follows a pattern of exposing the “masks” people wear in their lives to hide their true thoughts or feelings, from a thug not wanting to be one but doing so to fit in and seem cool, to a preacher of a church hiding his queerness, to a single mom exhausted from being one, eventually turning to Tyler himself and revealing some of his personal issues. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “Apple” is the eleventh track on Charli XCX’s sixth studio album, BRAT, releasing on June 7, 2024. On September 8, 2023, Charli posted a photo of a notebook with lyrics to “Apple” (called “The Apple” at the time). On April 2, 2024, Charli announced the song and confirmed that it would appear on BRAT via a voice memo sent to a small number of fans. This is the second official coproducer credit linked to Charli XCX, with “In The City”, a song featuring Sam Smith, released in October 19, 2023 being the first one. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: "My Love Mine All Mine" explores themes of love, longing, and the desire to possess and protect one's own emotions and affection. The lyrics evoke a sense of yearning for emotional connection and the fear of losing the love that the narrator holds dear. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: "Take Your Mask Off" is the ninth song on Tyler, The Creator's eighth studio album, CHROMAKOPIA. The song was first teased in a “CHROMAKOPIA VINYL” promotional video, uploaded to Tyler’s channel on October 22, 2024. A short part of this song was featured in among the other CHROMAKOPIA snippets. The song talks about Tyler's childhood growing up with his sexuality and hiding it, now encouraging himself to show his true self. This song includes a sample from People's Pleasure and Alive and Well's "A Feeling Inside". Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “Red Wine Supernova“ is the second track from Chappell Roan's debut studio album, The Rise and Fall of a Midwest Princess. The song was released on May 17, 2023, alongside an official music video. “I needed a campy gay girl song that captured the magic of having feelings for another girl. I packed the song with fun raunchy lyrics that make it feel like a night out flirting with the girl across the bar!“ — via Brooklyn Vegan Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “Balloon” is the 13th track off Tyler’s 8th studio album CHROMAKOPIA. The track sees Tyler reflecting on his success, ambition, and unique perspective on life. He reflects on his strong work ethic (“Why I work so hard? My soul profit”) and the desire for options with him expressing a dislike for small talk and superficiality. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “WILDFLOWER” from Billie Eilish’s third studio album HIT ME HARD AND SOFT is a deeply introspective track about navigating the emotional complexities of relationships, especially the lingering effects of a partner’s past. The song explores feelings of insecurity and the challenge of moving forward while still haunted by memories of a partner’s ex. In the lyrics, Billie reflects on the emotional weight of a previous relationship and wonders how much of it still lingers in her partner’s heart. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “WILDFLOWER” from Billie Eilish’s third studio album HIT ME HARD AND SOFT is a deeply introspective track about navigating the emotional complexities of relationships, especially the lingering effects of a partner’s past. The song explores feelings of insecurity and the challenge of moving forward while still haunted by memories of a partner’s ex. In the lyrics, Billie reflects on the emotional weight of a previous relationship and wonders how much of it still lingers in her partner’s heart. Key themes include heartbreak, jealousy, and vulnerability, as Billie questions whether she has crossed emotional lines and if her partner is also grappling with memories of their ex. The imagery in the song, such as “burning like a fever” and “crying on Valentine’s Day in a hotel,” evokes a sense of both physical and emotional intensity, highlighting the pain and guilt associated with these unspoken feelings. While it touches on personal insecurity, it is also a reflection on how past loves can quietly overshadow current relationships. Fans have connected the lyrics to Billie’s rumored relationships, making this one of the more candid and raw tracks from the album. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “THOUGHT I WAS DEAD” is the 11th song off of Tyler, The Creator's 8th album "Chromakopia". The song features fellow California rapper and TDE affiliate Schoolboy Q, however the vinyl version of the song features Atlanta rapper Playboi Carti instead. A teaser was posted to Tyler’s YouTube channel on October 30th 2024, along with an Instagram post confirming that it would be the 11th song on the album. Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: “Sweater Weather” is the lead single from The Neighbourhood’s debut EP I'm Sorry… and also features on their debut full-length album I Love You. “Sweater Weather” is one of The Neighbourhood’s most successful tracks, peaking at #14 on the Billboard Hot 100 charts in December 2013 and spent 11 weeks at #1 on the Alternative charts. The music video for the track reached 100 million views in December 2015, when the video was merely two years old. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Sweater Weather” is the lead single from The Neighbourhood’s debut EP I'm Sorry… and also features on their debut full-length album I Love You. “Sweater Weather” is one of The Neighbourhood’s most successful tracks, peaking at #14 on the Billboard Hot 100 charts in December 2013 and spent 11 weeks at #1 on the Alternative charts. The music video for the track reached 100 million views in December 2015, when the video was merely two years old. “Sweater Weather” is the first song the band had ever written together, composed during their first practice, around a year prior to its release. Frontman Jesse Rutherford recalled the historic day to Schon! in December 2015: “It was just a really special moment and it felt special before anybody said it was special. When we first wrote it and showed our families and some friends that we trusted their opinion around town or whatever, it was just a different reaction than we’d ever seen someone have to our music…” Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: "Kind of a funny story involving this song. There was this girl I knew once who was very beautiful. Maybe the most beautiful girl I’ve ever known personally. I tried my damnedest to make something happen but it just didn’t take and I gave up. Later, there was a party at her house and I stayed up very late until it was just me and this girl. And the sun was coming up and we were in her room listening to records. Something about watching the sun come up after staying up all night, the world just doesn’t feel real. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Kind of a funny story involving this song. There was this girl I knew once who was very beautiful. Maybe the most beautiful girl I’ve ever known personally. I tried my damnedest to make something happen but it just didn’t take and I gave up. Later, there was a party at her house and I stayed up very late until it was just me and this girl. And the sun was coming up and we were in her room listening to records. Something about watching the sun come up after staying up all night, the world just doesn’t feel real. I guess that’s why she decided that we could kiss, but only for a little bit and then she would go to sleep and I would have to leave. And that’s what happened. Afterwards, I quickly discovered that this isolated incident had not changed the situation between us and I shrugged and gave up again. Then, later, I was at her house and we got around to talking about writing songs. She asked if I’d ever written a song about her. Anyways, I told her I had just written a song about her called Lovers Rock. This was before the song was released but I played her a demo of it from my phone. It was sort of strange. I remember I was lying on her couch with my head in her lap and she was playing with my hair, and we just sat there and listened to the whole song in silence. When it was over I looked up at her and she said “I really like that one.” That was all." Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: "Juno" is a song by Sabrina Carpenter, from her sixth album Short n' Sweet. It was written by Sabrina Carpenter, John Ryan and Amy Allen. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Juno" is a song by Sabrina Carpenter, from her sixth album Short n' Sweet. It was written by Sabrina Carpenter, John Ryan and Amy Allen. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: "Kill Bill" is about the intensity of unresolved feelings in a relationship, particularly when the other person has moved on. The narrator is expressing her frustration, jealousy, and pain from her partner moving on and thus, fantasizes about getting revenge by literally killing them and their new partner. She realizes this isn't a wise plan and is struggling to accept that the relationship may not have been meant to be and has reached a point of no return. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Kill Bill" is about the intensity of unresolved feelings in a relationship, particularly when the other person has moved on. The narrator is expressing her frustration, jealousy, and pain from her partner moving on and thus, fantasizes about getting revenge by literally killing them and their new partner. She realizes this isn't a wise plan and is struggling to accept that the relationship may not have been meant to be and has reached a point of no return. Ultimately, her angry feelings power her to a place of acceptance and she's able to find closure in the knowledge that at least she put forth her best effort. The song gets its title from Kill Bill (2003–2004), a martial arts film duology that focuses on an assassin named the Bride and her quest to exact revenge on her ex-boyfriend through murder. Mirroring the films' plot, the song's lyrics discuss SZA's fantasy to kill an ex-boyfriend and his new girlfriend out of jealousy. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: The final track on Charli XCX’s eighth album, BRAT, “365” alludes directly to a never ending cycle of partying, occurring during all 365 days of year. The lyrics throughout utilize a double entendre of “bumpin' that” to refer to both music and cocaine. “365” links back to opening track “360” (notably, both titles share the same first two digits), which is sampled in this song, thus hinting at BRAT being a loop. This motif has also been seen before on Charli’s self titled 2019 album Charli Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: The final track on Charli XCX’s eighth album, BRAT, “365” alludes directly to a never ending cycle of partying, occurring during all 365 days of year. The lyrics throughout utilize a double entendre of “bumpin' that” to refer to both music and cocaine. “365” links back to opening track “360” (notably, both titles share the same first two digits), which is sampled in this song, thus hinting at BRAT being a loop. This motif has also been seen before on Charli’s self titled 2019 album Charli, where it begins with ”1999” and ends with ”2099”. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “LUNCH” by Billie Eilish is the lead single from her third studio album HIT ME HARD AND SOFT. The lyrics explore sexual themes, including expressions of sexual desire for another woman. This track emphasize physical attraction and lust, using food as a metaphor for consumption in a sexual context. Musically, the song follows a minimalist production, with Billie’s signature whispery, seductive vocal style, which adds to the sensual tone. The Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “LUNCH” by Billie Eilish is the lead single from her third studio album HIT ME HARD AND SOFT. The lyrics explore sexual themes, including expressions of sexual desire for another woman. This track emphasize physical attraction and lust, using food as a metaphor for consumption in a sexual context. Musically, the song follows a minimalist production, with Billie’s signature whispery, seductive vocal style, which adds to the sensual tone. The sexual nature of the lyrics paired with the sultry beats creates an atmosphere that is both daring and intimate, showcasing a more explicit side of Billie Eilish’s artistry. The song pushes boundaries both lyrically and emotionally, using suggestive language and metaphors to convey desire in a raw, unapologetic way. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: “Casual“ is an angsty slow-burn rock ballad bemoaning a situationship that is labeled as such, but clearly means more. In an interview with WECB.FM, Roan noted that the story is based off of a real life experience: “The whole song came from like, I heard from their friend that they were saying it was casual, when I didn’t think it was. I did not think it was, but apparently it was, so. And to me I was like, how could this be? How could this be casual?“ Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Casual“ is an angsty slow-burn rock ballad bemoaning a situationship that is labeled as such, but clearly means more. In an interview with WECB.FM, Roan noted that the story is based off of a real life experience: “The whole song came from like, I heard from their friend that they were saying it was casual, when I didn’t think it was. I did not think it was, but apparently it was, so. And to me I was like, how could this be? How could this be casual?“ While promoting the song on TikTok, Roan explained “I wrote this song bc I am legitimately so tired of love turning into situationships. And I know my friends are so tired about hearing about the sh*t show of my love life.” She elaborated further on the story of the song and music video for Coup de Main Magazine, saying: “The video is a representation of how I dream of a serious relationship with someone in my head, but in reality it may be far from that. Even if it may not feel like it, if there are no labels, it’s just casual.“ Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.

wiki.summary: Rumoured to be on Addison Rae’s upcoming project, ’Diet Pepsi’ stands as the first single after the release of her debut EP, AR, and marks the beginning of a new era for her discography after her collaboration with Charli XCX on the remixed rendition of Von Dutch. Sexual by nature, the track sees Addison talk about losing her ‘innocence’ in her respective partner’s ‘backseat’ Read more on Last.fm.

wiki.summary: "Like Him" is the 12th track on Tyler, The Creator's 8th studio album "CHROMAKOPIA". Its one of the few songs that were teased in the "CHROMAKOPIA VINYL" promotional video, uploaded to Tyler’s channel on October 22, 2024. The song was featured amongst the three "CHROMAKOPIA" snippets. This song seems to be Tyler talking about how he “Seems like him” him referring to his father who, he doesn’t personally know. Like if he was “Chasing a ghost” and “Not knowing where he is”. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Like Him" is the 12th track on Tyler, The Creator's 8th studio album "CHROMAKOPIA".
